headline: Tariff whiplash creates EM entry windows; conviction thesis intact
conviction_update: MELI recovered 3.1% this week as tariff pause relief lifted LatAm risk assets broadly — the Brazil-centric revenue base insulates it from direct US-China trade disruption better than most EM peers. PT $2,400 intact; the Mercado Pago fintech flywheel continues compounding irrespective of macro noise. NU added 2.4% on strong credit quality data out of Brazil — NPL ratios holding well below legacy bank incumbents despite the rate environment. Six-consecutive-quarter ARPU growth streak shows no signs of breaking; $18 PT remains conservative.

watchlist_movers.note: Surged 8.2% on fresh Milei reform momentum — IMF deal signals Argentina is threading the needle. Binary risk remains but the asymmetry is widening in the bull case.
New coverage initiated. Argentine IT services compounder with 70%+ US/EU revenue base — insulated from peso volatility. AI transformation budgets driving accelerating demand.
Quiet week operationally but tariff exemptions for defense-adjacent aerospace contracts could be a near-term catalyst. Electron launch cadence remains best-in-class.

market_context: 90-day tariff pause on non-China trade partners has stabilized EM currencies and opened re-entry windows across LatAm and South/SE Asia; frontier tech names recovering after a sharp April drawdown as rate expectations stabilize.
